2021.question: Which of the following Article of the Indian Constitution empowers the President to grant pardons? (SSC CGL 2021 Tier-1)
2021.explanation: Article 72 of the Constitution empowers the President to grant pardons, reprieves, respites or remissions of punishment.

2021.question: Who was the first Governor-General of Bengal? (SSC CGL 2021 Tier-1)
2021.explanation: Warren Hastings was the first Governor-General of Bengal, serving from 1772 to 1785.

2021.question: The 'Swaraj Party' was formed following the failure of which movement? (SSC CGL 2021 Tier-1)
2021.explanation: The Swaraj Party was established in 1923 after the withdrawal of the Non-Cooperation Movement.

2021.question: Which river is known as the 'Dakshin Ganga'? (SSC CGL 2021 Tier-1)
2021.explanation: Godavari is known as the Dakshin Ganga because of its large size and extent among the peninsular rivers.

2021.question: The Fundamental Duties of Indian citizens were added to the Constitution by which amendment? (SSC CGL 2021 Tier-1)
2021.explanation: Fundamental Duties were added by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976.

2024.explanation: The Basic Structure doctrine was established in 1973 Kesavananda Bharati case, limiting Parliament's amending power.

2024.question: Which river forms the boundary between India and Nepal for most of its length?
2024.explanation: The Kali River (Mahakali/Sharda) forms the boundary between India and Nepal for much of its course.

2024.question: The 'Doctrine of Lapse' was introduced by?
2024.explanation: The Doctrine of Lapse was an annexation policy devised by Lord Dalhousie (1848-56) to expand British territories.

2025.question: The headquarters of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is located in:
2025.explanation: The RBI headquarters is located in Mumbai, Maharashtra.

2022.question: Who is known as the 'Iron Man of Kumaon'?
2022.explanation: Badri Dutt Pandey (1882-1965) is known as the 'Iron Man of Kumaon' for his role in the freedom struggle and social reform in Kumaon.

2022.question: The Nanda Devi Biosphere Reserve was established in which year?
2022.explanation: The Nanda Devi Biosphere Reserve was established in 1988. It includes the Nanda Devi National Park and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

2022.question: Which is the state flower of Uttarakhand?
2022.explanation: Brahma Kamal (Saussurea obvallata) is the state flower of Uttarakhand. It blooms once a year and is considered sacred.

2022.question: The Kumbh Mela in Uttarakhand is held at which place?
2022.explanation: Haridwar is one of the four sites for Kumbh Mela in India. It is held every 12 years at the banks of the Ganga.

2022.question: Which pass connects India with Tibet through Uttarakhand?
2022.explanation: Lipu Lekh Pass (5,334 m) in Pithoragarh district connects India with Tibet. It is an ancient trade route and pilgrimage path to Kailash Mansarovar.

2022.question: The first Chief Minister of Uttarakhand was:
2022.explanation: Nityanand Swami was the first Chief Minister of Uttarakhand (then Uttaranchal) from November 2000 to October 2001.

2022.question: Which lake is known as 'Tal' in Nainital?
2022.explanation: Nainital Lake is locally called 'Naini Tal' where 'Tal' means lake.

2022.question: The Garhwali and Kumaoni languages belong to which language family?
2022.explanation: Garhwali and Kumaoni are Central Pahari languages of the Indo-Aryan family.

2022.question: The Uttarakhand Legislative Assembly has how many seats?
2022.explanation: Uttarakhand Vidhan Sabha has 70 seats, with 1 seat reserved for Anglo-Indian (abolished in 2020).

2022.question: Which is the highest motorable road in Uttarakhand?
2022.explanation: Mana Pass at 5,610m in Chamoli district is the highest motorable road in Uttarakhand.

2022.question: The Tungnath temple is the highest among the Panch Kedar. At what altitude is it located?
2022.explanation: Tungnath temple at 3,680 meters is the world's highest Shiva temple and the highest of Panch Kedar.

2022.question: The Govind Wildlife Sanctuary is located in which district?
2022.explanation: Govind Wildlife Sanctuary and National Park is located in Uttarkashi district.

2022.question: Which river originates from Yamunotri glacier?
2022.explanation: The Yamuna River originates from Yamunotri glacier at an altitude of 6,387 meters.

2022.question: The Nanda Devi peak was first climbed in which year?
2022.explanation: Nanda Devi was first climbed on 29 August 1936 by Bill Tilman and Noel Odell.

2022.question: The Corbett National Park was established in which year?
2022.explanation: Corbett National Park (originally Hailey National Park) was established in 1936 as India's first national park.

2022.question: The famous 'Har Ki Dun' valley is located in which district?
2022.explanation: Har Ki Dun (Valley of Gods) is located in Govind Wildlife Sanctuary, Uttarkashi district.

2023.question: The Chipko Movement started in which year in Uttarakhand?
2023.explanation: The Chipko Movement started in 1973 in Chamoli district of Uttarakhand. It was led by Sunderlal Bahuguna and Chandi Prasad Bhatt.

2023.question: Which lake is known as the 'Jewel of Kumaon'?
2023.explanation: Nainital Lake is known as the 'Jewel of Kumaon'. The town Nainital derives its name from this lake (Naini = eyes, Tal = lake).

2023.question: Which is the state bird of Uttarakhand?
2023.explanation: The Himalayan Monal (Monal Pheasant) is the state bird of Uttarakhand. It is also called Danphe and is known for its colorful plumage.

2023.question: Hemkund Sahib, a famous Sikh pilgrimage site, is located in which district?
2023.explanation: Hemkund Sahib is located at an altitude of 4,632 meters in Chamoli district. It is one of the highest Gurudwaras in the world.

2023.question: The Panch Prayag (Five Confluences) are located in which division of Uttarakhand?
2023.explanation: The Panch Prayag (Vishnuprayag, Nandprayag, Karnaprayag, Rudraprayag, Devprayag) are all located in the Garhwal division.

2023.question: Which folk dance is famous in the Kumaon region of Uttarakhand?
2023.explanation: Jhora is a popular folk dance of the Kumaon region. It is performed in a circle with men and women dancing together.

2023.question: The Alaknanda River originates from which glacier?
2023.explanation: The Alaknanda River originates from the Satopanth and Bhagirath Kharak glaciers near Badrinath in Chamoli district.

2023.question: Which is the largest district of Uttarakhand by area?
2023.explanation: Chamoli is the largest district of Uttarakhand by area, covering approximately 8,030 sq km. It is also known as the 'Land of God'.

2023.question: Which is the State Tree of Uttarakhand?
2023.explanation: Buransh (Rhododendron arboreum) is the State Tree as well as the source of the State Drink of Uttarakhand.

2023.question: The Kedarnath Wildlife Sanctuary is located in which district?
2023.explanation: Kedarnath Wildlife Sanctuary, spread over 975 sq km, is located in Rudraprayag and Chamoli districts.

2023.question: Which is the smallest district by area in Uttarakhand?
2023.explanation: Champawat is the smallest district of Uttarakhand by area, covering approximately 1,766 sq km.

2023.question: The Magh Mela is held at which place in Uttarakhand?
2023.explanation: The annual Magh Mela is held at Haridwar during the Hindu month of Magh (January-February).

2023.question: Which river is known as 'Dakshin Ganga'?
2023.explanation: The Godavari River is called Dakshin Ganga (Ganges of the South) as it is the largest peninsular river.

2023.question: The Askot Wildlife Sanctuary is famous for which animal?
2023.explanation: Askot Wildlife Sanctuary in Pithoragarh is known for Musk Deer and is a proposed Musk Deer Sanctuary.

2023.question: Who was the first Governor of Uttarakhand?
2023.explanation: Surjit Singh Barnala was the first Governor of Uttarakhand, serving from 2000 to 2003.

2023.question: The Pindari Glacier is located in which district?
2023.explanation: Pindari Glacier, one of the most accessible glaciers in the Himalayas, is located in Bageshwar district.

2023.question: Nainital Lake is which type of lake?
2023.explanation: Nainital Lake is a tectonic lake formed due to geological faulting in the region.

2023.question: The famous Nanda Devi Raj Jat Yatra is held after every how many years?
2023.explanation: Nanda Devi Raj Jat is a traditional pilgrimage held every 12 years in honor of Goddess Nanda Devi.

2024.question: Uttarakhand state was formed on which date?
2024.explanation: Uttarakhand (earlier called Uttaranchal) was carved out of Uttar Pradesh and became the 27th state of India on 9 November 2000.

2024.question: Which is the highest peak of Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: Nanda Devi (7,816 m) is the highest peak entirely within India and the highest in Uttarakhand. It is the second highest peak in India after Kangchenjunga.

2024.question: The famous Valley of Flowers is located in which district of Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: The Valley of Flowers National Park is located in Chamoli district of Uttarakhand. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

2024.question: Which river originates from Gangotri Glacier in Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: The Bhagirathi River (main stream of Ganga) originates from Gaumukh in the Gangotri Glacier. It meets Alaknanda at Devprayag to form the Ganga.

2024.question: How many districts are there in Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: Uttarakhand has 13 districts divided into two divisions - Garhwal (7 districts) and Kumaon (6 districts).

2024.question: The Char Dham Yatra in Uttarakhand includes which of the following?
2024.explanation: The Char Dham (Four Abodes) in Uttarakhand are Badrinath, Kedarnath, Gangotri, and Yamunotri - four sacred Hindu pilgrimage sites.

2024.question: Which is the capital city of Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: Dehradun is the interim capital of Uttarakhand, while Gairsain has been declared as the summer capital. Dehradun is the largest city in the state.

2024.question: The famous Corbett National Park is located in which district?
2024.explanation: Jim Corbett National Park, established in 1936, is India's oldest national park. It is located in Nainital district of Uttarakhand.

2024.question: Which of the following is the state animal of Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: The Musk Deer (Kasturi Mrig) is the state animal of Uttarakhand. It is found in the high altitude forests of the Himalayas.

2024.question: The Tehri Dam is built on which river?
2024.explanation: Tehri Dam is built on the Bhagirathi River near Tehri in Uttarakhand. It is one of the highest dams in the world at 260.5 meters.

2024.question: Which language is predominantly spoken in the Garhwal region of Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: Garhwali is the predominant language in the Garhwal division of Uttarakhand. It belongs to the Pahari language group.

2024.question: The Rajaji National Park is famous for which animal?
2024.explanation: Rajaji National Park, located in the Shivalik ranges, is famous for its elephant population. It was named after C. Rajagopalachari.

2024.question: Who was the first Chief Minister of Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: Nityanand Swami served as the first Chief Minister of Uttarakhand from November 2000 to October 2001.

2024.question: The Jim Corbett National Park is located in which district?
2024.explanation: Jim Corbett National Park is mainly in Nainital district, with some parts in Pauri Garhwal. It was established in 1936 as Hailey National Park.

2024.question: Which lake in Uttarakhand is known as the 'Lake of Mystery'?
2024.explanation: Roopkund Lake in Chamoli district is known as the 'Skeleton Lake' or 'Mystery Lake' due to human skeletal remains found there.

2024.question: Which is the State Animal of Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: The Musk Deer (Kasturi Mrig) is the State Animal of Uttarakhand. It is found in the Alpine forests of the Himalayas.

2024.question: Which bird is the State Bird of Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: The Himalayan Monal (Danphe) is the State Bird of Uttarakhand. It is known for its colorful plumage.

2024.question: The Chipko Movement started in which year?
2024.explanation: The Chipko Movement started in 1973 in Chamoli district of Uttarakhand. Sunderlal Bahuguna was a prominent leader of this movement.

2024.question: The famous Laxman Jhula is located in which city?
2024.explanation: Laxman Jhula is an iconic suspension bridge over the Ganga River in Rishikesh. It is named after Lord Laxman.

2024.question: Which is the largest district by area in Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: Chamoli is the largest district in Uttarakhand by area, covering approximately 8,030 sq km.

2024.question: Which is the oldest hill station in Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: Mussoorie, established as a hill station by the British in 1823, is the oldest hill station in Uttarakhand.

2024.question: The Ganga and Yamuna rivers originate from which glaciers respectively?
2024.explanation: The Ganga (Bhagirathi) originates from Gangotri Glacier and Yamuna originates from Yamunotri Glacier.

2024.question: Which famous fair is held at the confluence of Ganga and Yamuna in Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: The Kumbh Mela is held at Haridwar, one of the four sacred sites where the Kumbh rotates every 12 years.

2024.question: Which university is the oldest in Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: G.B. Pant University of Agriculture and Technology, Pantnagar, established in 1960, is the oldest university in Uttarakhand.

2025.question: Which is the longest river in Peninsular India?
2025.explanation: The Godavari is the longest river in Peninsular India, also known as the Dakshin Ganga.

2025.question: The headquarters of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is located in:
2025.explanation: The RBI headquarters is located in Mumbai, Maharashtra.

2025.question: Who was the first woman President of the Indian National Congress?
2025.explanation: Annie Besant became the first woman President of the Indian National Congress in 1917. Sarojini Naidu was the first Indian woman President.

2025.question: Who was the founder of the Maurya Empire?
2025.explanation: Chandragupta Maurya founded the Maurya Empire in 322 BCE.

2025.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Right to Equality?
2025.explanation: Article 14 guarantees equality before the law and equal protection of the laws.

2022.question: The Uttarakhand High Court is located in which city?
2022.explanation: The Uttarakhand High Court is located in Nainital, with a bench in Dehradun.

2022.question: Which is the longest river originating from Uttarakhand?
2022.explanation: The Ganga (formed at Devprayag by Bhagirathi and Alaknanda) is the longest river system originating from Uttarakhand.

2022.question: The Chaukori tea gardens are located in which district?
2022.explanation: Chaukori, famous for its tea gardens and Himalayan views, is located in Pithoragarh district.

2022.question: The Kainchi Dham temple is associated with which saint?
2022.explanation: Kainchi Dham near Nainital is the ashram of Neem Karoli Baba. It is visited by many devotees including Steve Jobs and Mark Zuckerberg.

2022.question: Which division of Uttarakhand has more districts?
2022.explanation: Garhwal division has 7 districts while Kumaon division has 6 districts, making a total of 13 districts.

2022.question: Which famous temple is located at Badrinath?
2022.explanation: Badrinath Temple is dedicated to Lord Vishnu and is one of the Char Dham.

2022.question: The Jaunsari tribe is found in which district?
2022.explanation: The Jaunsari tribe inhabits the Jaunsar-Bawar region in Dehradun district.

2022.question: Which river is formed by the confluence of Bhagirathi and Alaknanda?
2022.explanation: At Devprayag, the Bhagirathi and Alaknanda merge to form the holy River Ganga.

2022.question: The Rajaji National Park is named after?
2022.explanation: Rajaji National Park is named after C. Rajagopalachari, the last Governor-General of India.

2022.question: The famous Naini Mata Temple is located at?
2022.explanation: Naina Devi Temple is located on the northern shore of Nainital Lake.

2023.question: The Nanda Devi Raj Jat Yatra is held every:
2023.explanation: The Nanda Devi Raj Jat Yatra is held every 12 years. It is one of the longest and most arduous pilgrimages in India.

2023.question: Which is the coldest place in Uttarakhand?
2023.explanation: Munsiyari in Pithoragarh district is often considered the coldest inhabited place in Uttarakhand due to its high altitude.

2023.question: The famous Roopkund Lake is also known as:
2023.explanation: Roopkund Lake (5,029 m) in Chamoli is called 'Skeleton Lake' due to hundreds of human skeletons found around and inside the lake.

2023.question: Which district of Uttarakhand is known for apple production?
2023.explanation: Uttarkashi district is famous for apple production in Uttarakhand. The cold climate is suitable for apple cultivation.

2023.question: The famous Binsar Wildlife Sanctuary is located in which district?
2023.explanation: Binsar Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Almora district. It offers panoramic views of the Himalayan peaks.

2023.question: Which musical instrument is associated with Uttarakhand folk music?
2023.explanation: Dhol, Damau, and Hurka are all traditional musical instruments of Uttarakhand folk music, often played together during celebrations.

2023.question: Which is the longest river entirely in Uttarakhand?
2023.explanation: The Kali River (also known as Sharda) is the longest river that flows entirely within the boundaries of Uttarakhand.

2023.question: The Binsar Wildlife Sanctuary is located in which district?
2023.explanation: Binsar Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Almora district and is known for its Himalayan wildlife and bird species.

2023.question: The Golu Devta Temple is located at which place?
2023.explanation: The famous Golu Devta Temple is located at Champawat, where devotees offer bells and letters to the deity.

2023.question: Sumitranandan Pant was born in which place?
2023.explanation: Sumitranandan Pant, the renowned Hindi poet, was born in Kausani in Bageshwar district in 1900.

2023.question: The Munsyari is located in which district?
2023.explanation: Munsyari, known as 'Little Kashmir', is a hill station in Pithoragarh district near the Indo-Tibet border.

2023.question: Which dance form is NOT from Uttarakhand?
2023.explanation: Garba is a traditional dance from Gujarat. Langvir, Pandav Nritya, and Jhora are Uttarakhand folk dances.

2023.question: The Bhimtal Lake is located in which district?
2023.explanation: Bhimtal, the largest lake in Kumaon region, is located in Nainital district, about 22 km from Nainital town.

2023.question: Which national highway connects Rishikesh to Badrinath?
2023.explanation: National Highway 58 (now NH 7) connects Rishikesh to Mana Pass via Badrinath in Uttarakhand.

2023.question: The Tehri Hydro Power Plant has a capacity of?
2023.explanation: Tehri Dam Hydro Power Complex has a total installed capacity of 2400 MW (1000 MW from Tehri Dam and 1400 MW from Tehri PSP).

2023.question: The Uttarakhand High Court is located in?
2023.explanation: The Uttarakhand High Court is located in Nainital. It was established in 2000 when the state was formed.

2024.question: How many Lok Sabha seats are there in Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: Uttarakhand has 5 Lok Sabha constituencies: Tehri Garhwal, Garhwal, Almora, Nainital-Udham Singh Nagar, and Haridwar.

2024.question: The Asan Conservation Reserve is located near which city?
2024.explanation: Asan Conservation Reserve (Asan Barrage) is located near Dehradun. It is a Ramsar site and important for migratory birds.

2024.question: Which festival is celebrated in Uttarakhand to mark the start of harvest season?
2024.explanation: Harela is celebrated at the onset of monsoon and marks the beginning of the harvest season. It is celebrated on the first day of Shravan month.

2024.question: The Yamuna River originates from which glacier in Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: The Yamuna River originates from the Yamunotri Glacier at an elevation of 6,387 meters in the Bandarpoonch peak region.

2024.question: Which district of Uttarakhand shares border with Nepal?
2024.explanation: Pithoragarh district shares its eastern border with Nepal. It is also known as 'Little Kashmir' for its natural beauty.

2024.question: The 'Aipan' is a traditional art form of which region?
2024.explanation: Aipan is a traditional folk art of the Kumaon region. It involves creating decorative designs using rice paste on red ochre backgrounds.

2024.question: The Govind Wildlife Sanctuary is located in which district?
2024.explanation: Govind Wildlife Sanctuary and National Park is located in Uttarkashi district. It is home to snow leopard, musk deer, and bharal.

2024.question: Which of the following is NOT a Prayag in the Panch Prayag?
2024.explanation: The Panch Prayag are: Vishnuprayag, Nandprayag, Karnaprayag, Rudraprayag, and Devprayag. Haridwar is not one of them.

2024.question: Which is the winter capital of Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: Dehradun serves as the winter capital of Uttarakhand while Gairsain is the summer capital.

2024.question: The Corbett Tiger Reserve spans across how many districts?
2024.explanation: Corbett Tiger Reserve spans across Nainital and Pauri Garhwal districts in Uttarakhand.

2024.question: Who wrote the famous book 'My Kumaon'?
2024.explanation: Jim Corbett, the famous hunter and naturalist, wrote 'My Kumaon' based on his experiences in the Kumaon region.

2024.question: The Nanda Devi Biosphere Reserve was established in which year?
2024.explanation: The Nanda Devi Biosphere Reserve was established in 1988 and became a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

2024.question: Which river is known as 'Rishiganga' in Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: Rishi Ganga is a tributary of the Dhauli Ganga and flows through the Nanda Devi National Park area.

2024.question: The Auli ski resort is located in which district?
2024.explanation: Auli, the famous skiing destination, is located in Chamoli district at an altitude of about 2,500-3,000 meters.

2024.question: Which is the State Flower of Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: Brahma Kamal (Saussurea obvallata) is the State Flower of Uttarakhand. It blooms at high altitudes.

2024.question: The Kedarnath temple was built by which king?
2024.explanation: The Kedarnath temple is believed to have been built by Adi Shankaracharya in the 8th century.

2024.question: Which tribe is NOT found in Uttarakhand?
2024.explanation: Santhal tribe is primarily found in Jharkhand and West Bengal. Bhotiya, Tharu, and Jaunsari are Uttarakhand tribes.

2024.question: The Bhagirathi and Alaknanda meet at which place to form the Ganga?
2024.explanation: The Bhagirathi and Alaknanda rivers meet at Devprayag to form the River Ganga.
